qa_pairs.question: What is the default quota for Nearline space on the Nibi cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: The default quota for Nearline space on the Nibi cluster is 10 TB and 5000 files per group.

qa_pairs.question: Is Nearline space on the Nibi cluster Lustre-based?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, Nearline space on the Nibi cluster is Lustre-based.

qa_pairs.question: Is Nearline space on the Nibi cluster backed up?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, Nearline space on the Nibi cluster is backed up.

qa_pairs.question: Is Nearline space on the Nibi cluster purged?
qa_pairs.answer: No, Nearline space on the Nibi cluster is not purged.

qa_pairs.question: Is Nearline space available by default on the Nibi cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, Nearline space is available by default on the Nibi cluster.

qa_pairs.question: Is Nearline space on the Nibi cluster mounted on compute nodes?
qa_pairs.answer: No, Nearline space on the Nibi cluster is not mounted on compute nodes.

qa_pairs.question: When did the new Rapid Access Service (RAS) policies take effect?
qa_pairs.answer: The new Rapid Access Service (RAS) policies took effect on April 1, 2024.

qa_pairs.question: What do the new Rapid Access Service (RAS) policies allow for?
qa_pairs.answer: The new Rapid Access Service (RAS) policies allow for larger quotas for project and nearline spaces.

qa_pairs.question: Where can users find more details about the new Rapid Access Service (RAS) policies?
qa_pairs.answer: For more details, users can refer to the "Storage" section at [[Rapid Access Service]].

qa_pairs.question: How can users obtain quota changes larger than those permitted by the Rapid Access Service (RAS)?
qa_pairs.answer: Quota changes larger than those permitted by RAS will require an application to the annual [[Resource Allocation Competition]].

qa_pairs.question: What filesystem characteristics are typically listed for clusters like Narval and Rorqual?
qa_pairs.answer: The filesystem characteristics typically listed include 'Filesystem', 'Default Quota', 'Lustre-based', 'Backed up', 'Purged', 'Available by Default', and 'Mounted on Compute Nodes'.
